mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.2 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals supply-side capacity utilization that compresses input costs and boosts profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle expansions, with an out-of-sample hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 5.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 60% of observations meeting the CPIAUCSL above 5.0 and FEDFUNDS below 1.0 condition.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that sustains consumer spending and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day horizons, with a maximum drawdown exceeding 8% within the signal window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth reflect expanding economic output that directly lifts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows since 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates supply bottlenecks that erode real consumer purchasing power, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0.0 and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in greater than 70% of observations where industrial production contracts while inflation accelerates.
